\"Record, when relevant, the start and t: "Rekota, moo ho loketseng, letsatsi la qalo le la pheletso la kamano." (ISDF 6.3) Kenya letsatsi ka mokgwa oo o batlang hore le hlahe leqepheng la pontsho ya mosebetsi, o sebedise ditlhaloso kapa matshwao a mongolo ho bontsha ho se kgolwe ha ho hlokahala.
\"Use this note to record any other desc: "Sebedisa noto ena ho rekota tlhahisoleseding efe kapa efe ya tlhaloso e nkuwang e le bohlokwa empa e sa weleng ka hare ho ditlhaloso tsa dinoto tse ding." (RAD 1.8B21)

Record a citation to, and/or information: Rekoto a citation to, and/or tlhahisoleseding about a publication that is about or based on the use, study, or analysis of the unit of tlhaloso. Include references to e phatlaladitswe facsimiles or transcriptions. (ISAD 3.5.4)

Batla for an existing lebitso in the registry direkoto by typing the first few characters of the lebitso. Alternatively, request for a ntjha registry entry to be bopad. NARSSA Act 1996

When disabled: Finding Aid links are not:
When e thibetswe: Thuso ya ho Fumana links are not displayed, Thuso ya ho Fumana generation is
e thibetswe, and the 'Advanced Batla > Thuso ya ho Fumana' sefetisi is hidden.
When enabled the following text will app: When e buletswe the following text will appear in the %1% metadata section to describe how a mosebedisi may phihlello the original and preservation copy of the faele stored in a linked ya dijithale preservation system. The text appears in the "Ditumello" field. When e thibetswe, the "Ditumello" field is not displayed.

When e buletswe the following text will appear whenever a mosebedisi tries to jarolla a %1% master with an associated ditokelo statement where the Basis = copyright and the Restriction = boemoal. You can style and customize the text as in a static leqephe.
When enabled, an &quot;Upload limit&quot: When e buletswe, an "Moedi wa ho kenya" meter is displayed for authenticated basebedisi on the %1% sheba leqephe, and administrators can limit the disk space each %1% is allowed for %2% kenya faeles
When enabled, the copyright pop-up will: When e buletswe, the copyright pop-up will be applied to every %1%, regardless of whether there is an accompanying Ditokelo statement.
When set to &quot;no&quot;, the reposito: When set to "no", the setsi sa polokelo lebitso is excluded from certain displays because it will be too repetitive
When set to &quot;yes&quot;, the referen: When set to "yes", the khoutu ya tshupiso string will be built using the tlhahisoleseding ntho sesupo plus the sesupos of all its ancestors
